QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_53

question.text: At normal exit from NPS, what percentage of the lump sum withdrawal is tax-free?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At normal exit (age 60), up to 60% of the corpus can be withdrawn as lump sum, and this 60% is entirely tax-free. **The Trap:** The 60% refers to the lump sum portion that is tax-free. The remaining 40% goes to annuity purchase (also tax-free at purchase). The annuity income is taxable. **Cross-Exam Context:** NPS follows the EEE regime: Exempt at contribution (via 80CCD), Exempt on returns, Exempt at withdrawal (60% lump sum + 40% annuity purchase). Only annuity income is taxed. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Explain the EEE benefit to clients. NPS is one of the few instruments where the withdrawal is substantially tax-free.
